lessons.subject: Economic Management Sciences
lessons.title: Grade 7 The economy — Core teaching
learning_objectives: Explain core concepts related to the economy.
Use CAPS enquiry, practical, creative or source-work skills for the topic.
Apply the topic in familiar South African classroom and everyday contexts.
lessons.lesson_body:
In this lesson learners study the economy using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to the economy. Key vocabulary: economy, ECONOMIC, MANAGEMENT, SCIENCES. Use the map, source or timeline provided and explain how the evidence supports the answer. Grounding snippet: --- page 13 ---
ECONOMIC AND MANAGEMENT SCIENCES GRADES 7-9
8
CURRICULUM AND ASSESSMENT POLICY STATEMENT (CAPS)
SECTION 2: ECONOmIC AND mANAGEmENT SCIENCES
2.1 WHat is eConomiC and manaGement sCienCes?
The subject Econom Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.
lessons.explanation:

8

SECTION 2: ECONOmIC AND mANAGEmENT SCIENCES

The subject Econom Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: A map shows two towns linked by a river near the economy. Which source would best explain why settlers chose the site?
step_by_step_solution: List the physical features shown on the map.
Match each feature to a human need such as water or transport.
Choose the source that explains settlement reasons.
Write one sentence using evidence from the source.
worked_examples.answer: A historical source describing water access and farming explains the settlement choice.

worked_examples.question: Place these events about the economy in the correct order: policy change, protest march, community meeting.
step_by_step_solution: Identify which event happened first.
Decide which event caused the next event.
Arrange all events chronologically.
Check the order against the source summary.
worked_examples.answer: Community meeting, protest march, policy change.

practice_questions.question_text: Which source would best answer a question about the economy in the local area?

options.A: A fictional story with no historical details
options.B: A community interview or local archive record
options.C: A picture with no caption or date

practice_questions.explanation: Local sources provide relevant evidence.

practice_questions.question_text: When comparing places linked to the economy, what should learners examine first?

options.A: Only the colours used on the map
options.B: The title of the textbook page
options.C: Physical features and human activities shown on the map
options.D: The number of pages in the atlas

practice_questions.explanation: Maps show features and activities that explain differences.

practice_questions.question_text: Which timeline order is logical for an event about the economy?

options.A: Consequence, cause, event
options.B: Event, event, event with no order
options.C: Random order with no dates

practice_questions.explanation: Timelines show cause and effect over time.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: A community interview or local archive record
Physical features and human activities shown on the map
Cause, event, consequence

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for the economy and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Highlight the date, place and source before answering.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about the economy and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.EMS.1.1 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect the economy to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for the economy. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: social sciences.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about the economy without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports social sciences learning at home.

learning_objectives: Explain core concepts related to the economy.
Use CAPS enquiry, practical, creative or source-work skills for the topic.
Apply the topic in familiar South African classroom and everyday contexts.
lessons.lesson_body:
In this lesson learners study the economy using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to the economy. Key vocabulary: economy, ECONOMIC, MANAGEMENT, SCIENCES. Use the map, source or timeline provided and explain how the evidence supports the answer. Grounding snippet: --- page 13 ---
ECONOMIC AND MANAGEMENT SCIENCES GRADES 7-9
8
CURRICULUM AND ASSESSMENT POLICY STATEMENT (CAPS)
SECTION 2: ECONOmIC AND mANAGEmENT SCIENCES
2.1 WHat is eConomiC and manaGement sCienCes?
The subject Econom Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.
lessons.explanation:
In this lesson learners study the economy using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to the economy. Key vocabulary: economy, ECONOMIC, MANAGEMENT, SCIENCES. Use the map, source or timeline provided and explain how the evidence supports the answer. Grounding snippet: --- page 13 ---
ECONOMIC AND MANAGEMENT SCIENCES GRADES 7-9
8
CURRICULUM AND ASSESSMENT POLICY STATEMENT (CAPS)
SECTION 2: ECONOmIC AND mANAGEmENT SCIENCES
2.1 WHat is eConomiC and manaGement sCienCes?
The subject Econom Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: A map shows two towns linked by a river near the economy. Which source would best explain why settlers chose the site?
step_by_step_solution: List the physical features shown on the map.
Match each feature to a human need such as water or transport.
Choose the source that explains settlement reasons.
Write one sentence using evidence from the source.
worked_examples.answer: A historical source describing water access and farming explains the settlement choice.

worked_examples.question: Place these events about the economy in the correct order: policy change, protest march, community meeting.
step_by_step_solution: Identify which event happened first.
Decide which event caused the next event.
Arrange all events chronologically.
Check the order against the source summary.
worked_examples.answer: Community meeting, protest march, policy change.

practice_questions.question_text: Which source would best answer a question about the economy in the local area?

options.A: A fictional story with no historical details
options.B: A picture with no caption or date
options.C: An unrelated advertisement
options.D: A community interview or local archive record

practice_questions.explanation: Local sources provide relevant evidence.

practice_questions.question_text: When comparing places linked to the economy, what should learners examine first?

options.A: Physical features and human activities shown on the map
options.B: Only the colours used on the map
options.C: The title of the textbook page
options.D: The number of pages in the atlas

practice_questions.explanation: Maps show features and activities that explain differences.

practice_questions.question_text: Which timeline order is logical for an event about the economy?

options.A: Consequence, cause, event
options.B: Cause, event, consequence
options.C: Event, event, event with no order

practice_questions.explanation: Timelines show cause and effect over time.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: A community interview or local archive record
Physical features and human activities shown on the map
Cause, event, consequence

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for the economy and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Highlight the date, place and source before answering.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about the economy and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.EMS.1.1 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect the economy to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for the economy. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: social sciences.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about the economy without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports social sciences learning at home.

worked_examples.question: A map shows two towns linked by a river near goods and services. Which source would best explain why settlers chose the site?
step_by_step_solution: List the physical features shown on the map.
Match each feature to a human need such as water or transport.
Choose the source that explains settlement reasons.
Write one sentence using evidence from the source.
worked_examples.answer: A historical source describing water access and farming explains the settlement choice.

worked_examples.question: Place these events about goods and services in the correct order: policy change, protest march, community meeting.
step_by_step_solution: Identify which event happened first.
Decide which event caused the next event.
Arrange all events chronologically.
Check the order against the source summary.
worked_examples.answer: Community meeting, protest march, policy change.

practice_questions.question_text: Which source would best answer a question about goods and services in the local area?

options.A: A fictional story with no historical details

options.C: An unrelated advertisement
options.D: A community interview or local archive record

practice_questions.explanation: Local sources provide relevant evidence.

options.A: Physical features and human activities shown on the map
options.B: Only the colours used on the map

options.D: The number of pages in the atlas

practice_questions.explanation: Maps show features and activities that explain differences.

practice_questions.question_text: Which timeline order is logical for an event about goods and services?

options.A: Consequence, cause, event
options.B: Cause, event, consequence
options.C: Event, event, event with no order

practice_questions.explanation: Timelines show cause and effect over time.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: A community interview or local archive record
Physical features and human activities shown on the map
Cause, event, consequence

remediation_hints.example: Highlight the date, place and source before answering.

worked_examples.question: A map shows two towns linked by a river near assessment. Which source would best explain why settlers chose the site?
step_by_step_solution: List the physical features shown on the map.
Match each feature to a human need such as water or transport.
Choose the source that explains settlement reasons.
Write one sentence using evidence from the source.
worked_examples.answer: A historical source describing water access and farming explains the settlement choice.

worked_examples.question: Place these events about assessment in the correct order: policy change, protest march, community meeting.
step_by_step_solution: Identify which event happened first.
Decide which event caused the next event.
Arrange all events chronologically.
Check the order against the source summary.
worked_examples.answer: Community meeting, protest march, policy change.

worked_examples.question: A map shows two towns linked by a river near businesses and markets. Which source would best explain why settlers chose the site?
step_by_step_solution: List the physical features shown on the map.
Match each feature to a human need such as water or transport.
Choose the source that explains settlement reasons.
Write one sentence using evidence from the source.
worked_examples.answer: A historical source describing water access and farming explains the settlement choice.

worked_examples.question: Place these events about businesses and markets in the correct order: policy change, protest march, community meeting.
step_by_step_solution: Identify which event happened first.
Decide which event caused the next event.
Arrange all events chronologically.
Check the order against the source summary.
worked_examples.answer: Community meeting, protest march, policy change.

practice_questions.question_text: Which source would best answer a question about businesses and markets in the local area?

options.A: A fictional story with no historical details
options.B: A community interview or local archive record
options.C: A picture with no caption or date

practice_questions.explanation: Local sources provide relevant evidence.

practice_questions.question_text: When comparing places linked to businesses and markets, what should learners examine first?

options.A: Only the colours used on the map
options.B: The title of the textbook page
options.C: Physical features and human activities shown on the map
options.D: The number of pages in the atlas

practice_questions.explanation: Maps show features and activities that explain differences.

practice_questions.question_text: Which timeline order is logical for an event about businesses and markets?

options.A: Consequence, cause, event
options.B: Event, event, event with no order
options.C: Random order with no dates

practice_questions.explanation: Timelines show cause and effect over time.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: A community interview or local archive record
Physical features and human activities shown on the map
Cause, event, consequence

learning_objectives: Explain core concepts related to businesses and markets.
Use CAPS enquiry, practical, creative or source-work skills for the topic.
Apply the topic in familiar South African classroom and everyday contexts.
lessons.lesson_body: This visual lesson helps learners see businesses and markets with diagrams, models and labelled drawings. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to businesses and markets. Key vocabulary: Businesses, markets, reference, covers. Use the map, source or timeline provided and explain how the evidence supports the answer. Grounding snippet: CAPS reference 7.EMS.3.2 covers Businesses and markets. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.
lessons.explanation: This visual lesson helps learners see businesses and markets with diagrams, models and labelled drawings. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to businesses and markets. Key vocabulary: Businesses, markets, reference, covers. Use the map, source or timeline provided and explain how the evidence supports the answer. Grounding snippet: CAPS reference 7.EMS.3.2 covers Businesses and markets. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: A map shows two towns linked by a river near businesses and markets. Which source would best explain why settlers chose the site?
step_by_step_solution: List the physical features shown on the map.
Match each feature to a human need such as water or transport.
Choose the source that explains settlement reasons.
Write one sentence using evidence from the source.
worked_examples.answer: A historical source describing water access and farming explains the settlement choice.

worked_examples.question: Place these events about businesses and markets in the correct order: policy change, protest march, community meeting.
step_by_step_solution: Identify which event happened first.
Decide which event caused the next event.
Arrange all events chronologically.
Check the order against the source summary.
worked_examples.answer: Community meeting, protest march, policy change.

practice_questions.question_text: Which source would best answer a question about businesses and markets in the local area?

options.A: A community interview or local archive record
options.B: A fictional story with no historical details
options.C: A picture with no caption or date

practice_questions.explanation: Local sources provide relevant evidence.

practice_questions.question_text: When comparing places linked to businesses and markets, what should learners examine first?

options.A: Only the colours used on the map
options.B: Physical features and human activities shown on the map
options.C: The title of the textbook page
options.D: The number of pages in the atlas

practice_questions.explanation: Maps show features and activities that explain differences.

practice_questions.question_text: Which timeline order is logical for an event about businesses and markets?

options.A: Consequence, cause, event
options.B: Event, event, event with no order
options.C: Cause, event, consequence

practice_questions.explanation: Timelines show cause and effect over time.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: A community interview or local archive record
Physical features and human activities shown on the map
Cause, event, consequence

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for businesses and markets and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Highlight the date, place and source before answering.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about businesses and markets and teach it to a partner using the visual approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.EMS.3.2 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect businesses and markets to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for businesses and markets. Use the visual variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: social sciences.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about businesses and markets without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports social sciences learning at home.

options.B: The title of the textbook page
options.C: Physical features and human activities shown on the map
options.D: The number of pages in the atlas

practice_questions.explanation: Maps show features and activities that explain differences.

practice_questions.question_text: Which timeline order is logical for an event about assessment?

options.A: Consequence, cause, event
options.B: Event, event, event with no order
options.C: Random order with no dates

practice_questions.explanation: Timelines show cause and effect over time.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: A community interview or local archive record
Physical features and human activities shown on the map
Cause, event, consequence

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for assessment and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Highlight the date, place and source before answering.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about assessment and teach it to a partner using the real world sa approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.EMS.3.4 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect assessment to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for assessment. Use the real world sa variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: social sciences.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about assessment without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports social sciences learning at home.

worked_examples.question: A map shows two towns linked by a river near assessment. Which source would best explain why settlers chose the site?
step_by_step_solution: List the physical features shown on the map.
Match each feature to a human need such as water or transport.
Choose the source that explains settlement reasons.
Write one sentence using evidence from the source.
worked_examples.answer: A historical source describing water access and farming explains the settlement choice.

worked_examples.question: Place these events about assessment in the correct order: policy change, protest march, community meeting.
step_by_step_solution: Identify which event happened first.
Decide which event caused the next event.
Arrange all events chronologically.
Check the order against the source summary.
worked_examples.answer: Community meeting, protest march, policy change.

worked_examples.question: A map shows two towns linked by a river near revision. Which source would best explain why settlers chose the site?
step_by_step_solution: List the physical features shown on the map.
Match each feature to a human need such as water or transport.
Choose the source that explains settlement reasons.
Write one sentence using evidence from the source.
worked_examples.answer: A historical source describing water access and farming explains the settlement choice.

worked_examples.question: Place these events about revision in the correct order: policy change, protest march, community meeting.
step_by_step_solution: Identify which event happened first.
Decide which event caused the next event.
Arrange all events chronologically.
Check the order against the source summary.
worked_examples.answer: Community meeting, protest march, policy change.

practice_questions.question_text: Which source would best answer a question about revision in the local area?

options.A: A fictional story with no historical details
options.B: A picture with no caption or date
options.C: An unrelated advertisement
options.D: A community interview or local archive record

practice_questions.explanation: Local sources provide relevant evidence.

practice_questions.question_text: When comparing places linked to revision, what should learners examine first?

options.A: Physical features and human activities shown on the map
options.B: Only the colours used on the map
options.C: The title of the textbook page
options.D: The number of pages in the atlas

practice_questions.explanation: Maps show features and activities that explain differences.

practice_questions.question_text: Which timeline order is logical for an event about revision?

options.A: Consequence, cause, event
options.B: Cause, event, consequence
options.C: Event, event, event with no order

practice_questions.explanation: Timelines show cause and effect over time.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: A community interview or local archive record
Physical features and human activities shown on the map
Cause, event, consequence

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for revision and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Highlight the date, place and source before answering.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 7 example about revision and teach it to a partner using the story approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 7.EMS.4.2 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect revision to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.

Subject focus: social sciences.

worked_examples.question: A map shows two towns linked by a river near assessment. Which source would best explain why settlers chose the site?
step_by_step_solution: List the physical features shown on the map.
Match each feature to a human need such as water or transport.
Choose the source that explains settlement reasons.
Write one sentence using evidence from the source.
worked_examples.answer: A historical source describing water access and farming explains the settlement choice.

worked_examples.question: Place these events about assessment in the correct order: policy change, protest march, community meeting.
step_by_step_solution: Identify which event happened first.
Decide which event caused the next event.
Arrange all events chronologically.
Check the order against the source summary.
worked_examples.answer: Community meeting, protest march, policy change.
